data.title: Distinct Healthcare Moves Shareholder Communications Online Under New HKEX Rules
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited ( (HK:2677) ) has provided an update.

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited has shifted to electronic dissemination of its corporate communications in line with amended Hong Kong listing rules effective 31 December 2023. Future documents such as annual and interim reports, meeting notices, listing documents and circulars will be published on the company’s website and the HKEX news platform in both English and Chinese, replacing routine distribution of printed copies.

The company will no longer send separate notifications when new documents are posted online, instead urging non-registered shareholders to monitor the websites proactively. Investors who still prefer hard copies can submit a request form or written request, with such elections remaining valid only until the end of the following financial year, signaling a clear push toward digital communications and reduced paper-based investor materials.

More about Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2677. The group operates in the healthcare sector, providing medical and related clinical services, with its investor relations and regulatory disclosures focused on shareholders in the Hong Kong capital market.

Average Trading Volume: 136,097

data.title: Yue Yuen Posts Lower March and First-Quarter 2026 Revenues Across Manufacturing and Retail
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) ( (HK:0551) ) has provided an announcement.

Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) reported unaudited net consolidated operating revenue of USD 604.0 million for March 2026 and USD 1.99 billion for the first three months of the year, reflecting year-on-year declines. Manufacturing revenue fell 11.1% in March and 5.5% year-to-date, while Pou Sheng’s revenue, measured in RMB, declined 6.3% for the month and 1.1% year-to-date, resulting in overall group revenue contractions of 8.2% for March and 2.2% year-to-date.

The announcement, aligned with disclosure obligations in Hong Kong and Taiwan due to Yue Yuen’s status as a PCC subsidiary, signals ongoing top-line pressure across both its manufacturing and China retail operations. The sustained revenue declines may indicate continued demand softness or competitive challenges in the footwear sector, with implications for margins, capacity utilization, and investor sentiment toward the group’s near-term performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0551) stock is a Sell with a HK$7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) stock, see the HK:0551 Stock Forecast page.

More about Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings)

Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) Limited is a Hong Kong-listed footwear group and a subsidiary of Taiwan-listed Pou Chen Corporation, which holds a controlling interest through subsidiaries. The company operates a manufacturing business and, via its retail arm Pou Sheng in China, participates in the broader athletic and casual footwear value chain and related retail markets.

data.title: Toyo Engineering Eliminates Class A Preferred Shares Through Full Conversion
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Toyo Engineering Corporation ( (JP:6330) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Toyo Engineering Corporation announced that shareholders Integral Team Limited Partnership and Innovation Alpha Team L.P. have fully converted their 500,100 Class A preferred shares into an equivalent number of common shares, eliminating all outstanding Class A preferred shares. The transaction increases the company’s common shares to 58,828,807, consolidates its equity structure, and may simplify its capital base and governance profile for existing and prospective investors.

The removal of Class A preferred shares, many of which were held as treasury shares, streamlines Toyo Engineering’s share composition without altering total equity, potentially improving transparency around ownership and capital structure. This move may also enhance liquidity in the common stock and align shareholder interests more closely around a single class of shares, which can be viewed positively by the market and long-term stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:6330) stock is a Hold with a Yen2276.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Toyo Engineering Corporation stock, see the JP:6330 Stock Forecast page.

More about Toyo Engineering Corporation

Toyo Engineering Corporation is a Japan-based engineering company listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, specializing in the design and construction of industrial plants and related engineering services. The company serves domestic and international clients, positioning itself as a key player in large-scale infrastructure and energy-related projects.

data.title: Bank of Ireland Executives Disclose Share Deals Under 2023 Equity Plan
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bank Of Ireland Group plc ( (DE:BIRG) ) has shared an update.

Bank of Ireland has disclosed a series of transactions by senior executives under the EU Market Abuse Regulation, involving the acquisition of ordinary shares through the group’s 2023 Fixed Share Allowance Plan. Group COO Ciarán Coyle, Group Chief People Officer Matt Elliott, Corporate and Commercial Banking CEO John Feeney, Retail UK CEO Gail Goldie and Group Chief Risk Officer Rhys Kiff all received share awards and sold portions on Euronext Dublin to settle associated tax and social security liabilities.

The disclosures highlight ongoing use of equity-based remuneration to align leadership incentives with shareholder interests, while the immediate partial disposals underscore the tax-driven nature of much of the dealing rather than any directional view on the bank’s prospects. The transactions, all dated 1 April 2026 and executed around €15.54–€15.63 per share, provide investors with additional transparency on insider dealings at the top of the Irish lender.

More about Bank Of Ireland Group plc

Bank of Ireland Group plc is a leading Irish financial services provider operating across retail, corporate and commercial banking, with listings on Euronext Dublin. The group offers a broad range of banking products and services to personal, business and institutional customers in Ireland and the UK, including through its Retail UK and Corporate and Commercial Banking divisions.

data.title: Salter Brothers’ Small-Cap Fund Hit by Sell-Off but Sees Rebound Potential
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Salter Brothers Emerging Companies Ltd. ( (AU:SB2) ) has provided an announcement.

Salter Brothers Emerging Companies reported a pre-tax net tangible asset value of $0.798 per share and post-tax NTA of $0.920 as at 31 March 2026, with its shares trading at a 32.61% discount to NTA and delivering an annualised dividend yield of 6.45%. The portfolio fell 13.96% in March amid a sharp sell-off in Australian small and micro caps driven by Middle East tensions, rising interest rates and a rotation into large caps, but the manager argues that depressed valuations and oversold conditions could set up a rebound once macro uncertainty eases.

The company’s holdings remain concentrated in industrials and technology, with 80.1% of assets in listed names and 9.4% in cash, and the board recently declared a fully franked interim dividend of 2 cents per share. Management highlighted ongoing operational momentum in select feature stocks such as Felix Group and Acrow, and noted that small-cap forward P/E multiples now sit about 15% below their 10-year average, underscoring the potential upside for investors prepared to look through current volatility.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SB2) stock is a Hold with a A$0.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Salter Brothers Emerging Companies Ltd. stock, see the AU:SB2 Stock Forecast page.

More about Salter Brothers Emerging Companies Ltd.

Salter Brothers Emerging Companies Ltd is an ASX-listed investment company focused on small and micro-cap equities, with a portfolio spanning 35 holdings across industrials, information technology, consumer sectors, health care and financials. The fund targets emerging companies, maintains a mix of listed, unlisted investments and cash, and is structured to comply with Australia’s Significant Investor Visa emerging companies requirements.

Reckitt ( (GB:RKT) ) has provided an announcement.

Reckitt Benckiser Group has repurchased 154,000 of its ordinary shares on 7 April 2026 from Deutsche Bank AG’s London branch under a shareholder-approved buyback programme, with prices ranging between 5,122.00p and 5,254.00p and a volume-weighted average of 5,177.89p. The shares bought back will be held in treasury, bringing total treasury holdings to 30,791,147 shares and leaving 643,214,605 shares in issue, a change that affects the company’s voting rights denominator for investors tracking disclosure thresholds.

By increasing its treasury share position, Reckitt is refining its capital structure and potentially enhancing earnings per share over time, a move that may be viewed as a signal of management’s confidence in the business. The updated voting rights figure provides clarity for shareholders and market participants under UK transparency rules, ensuring accurate calculation of ownership stakes and regulatory disclosure obligations.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RKT is a Neutral.

data.title: Tempest Minerals Exits Elephant JV to Refocus on Core Projects
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tempest Minerals Limited ( (AU:TEM) ) has provided an update.

Tempest Minerals has exited the Elephant Project joint venture, ending all current and future financial and operational commitments to the asset and delivering immediate capital savings. The move follows a strategic review and reflects a shift toward a more focused portfolio centred on its existing Western Australian projects, including the Range gold discovery at Mt Magnet and newly announced, more advanced projects in New Zealand.

By reallocating capital and resources away from the Elephant Project, Tempest intends to accelerate exploration and development across its priority assets and strengthen its pipeline of near-term opportunities. The decision signals a tightening of strategic focus that could improve operational efficiency and potentially enhance the company’s competitive positioning in the junior mining sector, with further updates on its refocused portfolio expected in due course.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:TEM) stock is a Hold with a A$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tempest Minerals Limited stock, see the AU:TEM Stock Forecast page.

More about Tempest Minerals Limited

Tempest Minerals Ltd is an Australian-based mineral exploration company with a diversified portfolio of projects in Western Australia, where it is progressing an iron ore project toward development while also exploring for precious, base and energy metals. The company is led by an experienced board and management team and aims to maximise shareholder value through focused, data-driven and risk-weighted exploration and development of its assets.
